source: Met Police FOI disclosures; Big Brother Watch; Liberty; press reporting; Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026); 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid
notes: All records sourced from publicly available documents. data_quality reflects confidence in location/date accuracy: confirmed = primary source, approximate = derived from adjacent evidence, unverified = single secondary source only. Records lfr-009 through lfr-031 derive from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026) cross-referenced with the 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid PDF; additional fields (vendor, outcome_faces_scanned, threshold, outcome_false_alerts) present only on these records.

deployments.notes: One of the earliest documented Met LFR mobile deployments. Romford Market is a major street market, high footfall.
Threshold: 0.6. Watchlist: 5816
Threshold: 0.6. Watchlist: 7316
Threshold: 0.6. Watchlist: 7292
Deployed during BLM protest period (June 2020). Westfield Stratford City and Stratford Shopping Centre are part of the same complex. See also lfr-005 (2023 deployment at same location).

Threshold: 0.6. Watchlist: 6793

deployments.stated_purpose: Crowd safety, wanted persons, King's Coronation
deployments.notes: King's Coronation of Charles III. Largest single-day LFR deployment in this dataset by faces scanned (89,000). Outcome data from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026) cross-referenced with 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid. FA_Rate 0.
Outcome data from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026) cross-referenced with 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid. FA_Rate 0. Same geographic cluster as lfr-003 (2020) and lfr-005 (Sep 2023).
Date corrected from source (was listed as 11/07/23, confirmed 2023-07-11). Outcome data from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026) cross-referenced with 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid. FA_Rate 0.

deployments.stated_purpose: Crowd safety, wanted persons, Notting Hill Carnival
deployments.notes: Notting Hill Carnival, day 1 per source data. Note: 2023 Carnival main days were 27-28 August; 2023-08-25 may represent pre-event deployment. Outcome data from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026) cross-referenced with 2023-2024 LFR deployment grid. FA_Rate 0. See also lfr-019.

deployments.stated_purpose: Crowd safety, wanted persons, Notting Hill Carnival
deployments.notes: Notting Hill Carnival, day 2 per source data. Date flagged: 2023-09-01 falls after actual 2023 Carnival dates (27-28 August); may be erroneous or a post-event deployment. Data quality set to approximate pending date verification. Outcome data from Garbett GLA report (Feb 2026). FA_Rate 0. See also lfr-018.
Same physical complex as lfr-003 (2020). 167m separation in coordinates reflects different entrance points of the same mall.

Shaun Thompson misidentification case. Thompson was stopped and detained after LFR matched him with his brother (on bail for a violent offence). LFR identification was erroneous. Thompson and Silkie Carlo (Big Brother Watch) became claimants in Liberty v Metropolitan Police (High Court, 21 Apr 2026, challenge dismissed; appeal indicated). Date and exact location approximate.

Page 8 of Met LFR deployment grid PDF. Duration: 6h 21m. Missed by initial Garbett-GLA extraction (covering same source) — discovered via photographer eyewitness corroboration (Jared Krauss, photo EXIF 2024-11-01) cross-referenced back to the Met PDF. Triggers full multi-model re-extraction of the deployment grid.

deployments.location_name: Croydon High Street (static cameras, north and south ends)
deployments.notes: Oct 2025 to Mar 2026 pilot: 173 arrests (approx 1 per 35 min); 470,000+ faces scanned; 1 false alert; crime down 10.5%; violence against women down 21%. Made permanent Mar 2026. First Met permanent static LFR installation. Croydon is also one of London's most deprived boroughs. Demographic implications documented by Zoe Garbett GLA Feb 2026.
